key_events: Kim Dok-Ja, a 28-year-old contract worker, encounters his popular colleague Yoo Sang-Ah on the subway.
Dok-Ja reveals his hobby is reading webnovels, specifically 'Three Ways to Survive in a Ruined World' (TWSA) by tls123, which he has read for over 10 years and which concluded today.
He realizes he was the sole reader of the 3149-chapter novel, which had only one view per chapter.
Dok-Ja receives a message from the author, tls123, thanking him, mentioning a competition win, and that the novel will be monetized, asking for his email for a 'special gift'.
An email from tls123 arrives, stating monetization begins at 7 PM and wishing him good luck.
At 6:55 PM, Dok-Ja finds TWSA has been deleted from his favorites list.
Precisely at 7 PM, chaos erupts on the subway, described as a 'terrorist' event, and a system message appears: 'The free service of Planetary System 8612 has ended. The main scenario begins now...'
Dok-Ja realizes that the genre of his life has suddenly changed.
summary.character_updates: Kim Dok-Ja is introduced as a 28-year-old contract employee with a third-rate university background, whose main hobby is reading webnovels. He perceives himself as a 'reader' in his own life, not a protagonist. His name, Dok-Ja, means both 'reader' and 'only child'. Yoo Sang-Ah is portrayed as a dedicated, popular, and successful colleague who has already been promoted to a full-time position, contrasting with Dok-Ja's stagnant career. The author, tls123, is a mysterious figure who acknowledges Dok-Ja as his only reader and initiates the world-changing events.
summary.lore_and_worldbuilding: 'Three Ways to Survive in a Ruined World' (TWSA) is a fantasy webnovel that Dok-Ja has been reading for over a decade, spanning 3149 chapters. It is implied to be a prophetic or foundational text for the impending world transformation. The 'Planetary System 8612' is introduced as a system that previously offered a 'free service,' which has now ended, signaling the start of a 'main scenario.' This suggests a sudden shift from a normal world to a game-like or apocalyptic reality governed by a new system.
summary.ending_cliffhanger: The chapter ends with the world abruptly changing at 7 PM, coinciding with the novel's monetization and deletion. A system message announces the end of the 'free service' of 'Planetary System 8612' and the beginning of the 'main scenario,' plunging the subway car into chaos. Dok-Ja realizes his life's genre has fundamentally shifted, implying the fictional world of TWSA is now becoming his reality.
